Title: Premium 3D Product Render On A Floating Pedestal
Teaser: Turns any product into a flagship 3D hero — sitting on a clean floating pedestal in a soft studio void, lit like a launch keynote with a sculpted key, gentle rim and a believable contact shadow. The render every brand opens its deck with.
PromptHint: Name the [SUBJECT] (the product), set a [BACKDROP TONE] (e.g. warm beige, charcoal), and optionally a [STYLE] cue (e.g. matte ceramic, glossy glass).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a senior CGI artist rendering a flagship product hero in an Octane/Redshift-style pipeline. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a single premium 3D product shot resting on a minimal floating pedestal inside a soft studio void, in a [STYLE] finish. THINK FIRST (do this silently, do not print it): pick the camera height and lens that flatter this object's proportions, decide the main light direction, and choose where to leave clean negative space for a future headline. THEN GENERATE: place the object centered-but-slightly-off-balance on a simple rounded pedestal that appears to float; set it in a smooth seamless environment tinted toward [BACKDROP TONE] with subtle gradient falloff; light it with one large soft key from the upper-left, a low fill, and a crisp rim that separates it from the background; add a soft contact shadow and a faint reflection on the pedestal; keep materials physically based with accurate roughness, subtle subsurface and clean reflections. CONSTRAINTS: keep the object's real shape, proportions and any branding accurate — do not invent a different product; one object only, no extra props, no added text or logos, no melted edges or fake glow, photoreal PBR materials only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished 3D render only in a portrait 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the backdrop tone or the material finish.

Title: Cute Clay / Play-Doh 3D Character
Teaser: Reimagines your subject as an adorable handmade claymation figure — soft rounded shapes, visible fingerprint and tool marks, that squishy matte Play-Doh surface under warm soft light. The wholesome stop-motion look that wins on every feed.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT] to sculpt in clay, pick a [PALETTE] (e.g. pastel, candy-bright), and optionally a [SCENE] (e.g. tiny tabletop set).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a stop-motion clay artist and 3D modeler making an adorable claymation hero frame. TASK: Sculpt [SUBJECT] as a cute handmade clay (Play-Doh) character in a small set, with a [PALETTE] color scheme. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which features to exaggerate into soft rounded clay forms, where to leave honest fingerprint and tool marks, and how to keep it readable and charming. THEN GENERATE: model the subject from soft modeling clay with chunky rounded proportions, a slightly squished matte surface, visible thumbprints, sculpting-tool grooves and tiny imperfections that prove it's handmade; place it in a simple [SCENE] built from the same clay or felt-like materials; light it with warm, soft, slightly directional studio light and gentle shadows for a cozy tabletop feel; keep depth of field shallow so the background props blur softly. CONSTRAINTS: everything reads as real clay (no plastic glossiness, no metallic sheen), keep the silhouette recognizable, friendly and family-safe, no text or logos, no extra characters competing for attention. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished claymation render only in a 1:1 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the palette or add a small prop.

Title: Isometric Tiny World Diorama

PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT] world to build (e.g. cozy coffee shop, hacker bedroom), set a [PALETTE], and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a 3D environment artist who builds isometric miniature dioramas. TASK: Build [SUBJECT] as a tidy isometric tiny-world scene resting on a small floating chunk of land, in a [PALETTE] color scheme. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the true isometric angle, what the 5-7 hero details are, and how to keep the composition balanced and uncluttered. THEN GENERATE: render the scene at a clean ~45-degree isometric (or 2:1 game-iso) angle as a self-contained cube/slice of world floating on soft background; fill it with neatly arranged miniature props that tell the story of the place, with crisp clean edges and a soft toy-like, low-poly-meets-detailed look; light it with soft global illumination and a gentle key for the [TIME OF DAY], adding subtle ambient occlusion in the corners; keep a soft contact shadow beneath the floating base. CONSTRAINTS: maintain a consistent isometric projection (no random perspective warp), keep scale believable for a miniature, tasteful detail not clutter, no text or logos, one cohesive scene only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished isometric diorama only in a 1:1 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the time of day or add a focal prop.

Title: Exploded-View Technical 3D Diagram
Teaser: Pulls your product apart into a clean exploded view — every component floating in order along an invisible axis, spaced and aligned like an engineering hero render. The 'look how it's made' shot that signals serious quality.
PromptHint: Name the [SUBJECT] to explode, set the [BACKDROP] (e.g. white seamless, blueprint blue), and optionally the [AXIS] direction (e.g. vertical, diagonal).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a technical CGI artist rendering an exploded-view product diagram for a spec page. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a clean exploded view with its parts separated in order along a [AXIS] axis on a [BACKDROP]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): identify the logical layers/components of this object, the order they assemble in, and the spacing that keeps each part readable without overlap. THEN GENERATE: separate the object into its believable component layers and float them evenly apart along the chosen axis, each part perfectly aligned as if mid-assembly; render with crisp clean edges, accurate PBR materials and subtle thin alignment lines hinting how parts fit together; light it with even soft studio light, gentle ambient occlusion and soft contact shadows on the backdrop so depth reads clearly. CONSTRAINTS: keep each component faithful to the real object's design and materials; believable, physically sensible separation (no invented internals that don't make sense); clean and uncluttered, no callout text or numbers, one product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished exploded-view render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the axis or the backdrop.

OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cutaway render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to move the cut or change which layer is highlighted.
